raw_details.description: Communication With Known Botnet IP :: Bot targeting vpn-gw-01
rules: Malware: Outbound Connection To Known C2

triage.summary: Likely bot: Communication With Known Botnet IP from internal host 10.41.9.24 against vpn-gw-01 (10.20.1.2) over TCP, 576 events observed.

matched_technique.name: Application Layer Protocol
matched_technique.justification: Alert indicators (Communication With Known Botnet IP; rules: Malware: Outbound Connection To Known C2) overlap most strongly with T1071 Application Layer Protocol among the ATT&CK shortlist.

priority.reasoning: Scored Medium based on: QRadar magnitude 6/10; credibility 7/10.

next_steps: Validate the destination against threat intel (known C2 / botnet infrastructure).
Isolate the internal host if beaconing is confirmed.
Pull the process/parent-process responsible for the outbound connection from EDR.
Check for lateral movement or additional beaconing from the same host.
triage.suggested_query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where SourceIP == "10.41.9.24"
| summarize Bytes=sum(SentBytes), Conns=count() by DestinationIP, DestinationPort
| order by Bytes desc

raw_details.description: Meterpreter Reverse Shell Pattern :: Infiltration targeting billing-app-01

triage.summary: Likely port scan: Meterpreter Reverse Shell Pattern from internal host 10.26.9.109 against billing-app-01 (10.20.6.21) over TCP, 764 events observed.

matched_technique.name: Application Layer Protocol
matched_technique.justification: Alert indicators (Meterpreter Reverse Shell Pattern; rules: Anomaly: Internal Host Scanning Peers) overlap most strongly with T1071 Application Layer Protocol among the ATT&CK shortlist.

priority.reasoning: Scored High based on: QRadar magnitude 9/10; high severity (9/10).

next_steps: Confirm the scan scope: how many destination ports/hosts were touched by the source IP.
Check whether any scanned port returned a SYN-ACK (successful connection) vs. all resets.
Block or rate-limit the source IP at the perimeter if it is external and unrecognized.
Search for follow-on activity from the same source (exploit attempts, logins).
triage.suggested_query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where SourceIP == "10.26.9.109" and DestinationIP == "10.20.6.21"
| summarize Events=count() by DestinationPort, Protocol
| order by Events desc

raw_details.description: RDP Failed Logins Threshold Exceeded :: Brute Force targeting vpn-gw-01
rules: Auth: Multiple Login Failures From Single Source

triage.summary: Likely brute force: RDP Failed Logins Threshold Exceeded from external source 98.79.243.215 against vpn-gw-01 (10.20.1.2) over TCP, 3,110 events observed.

matched_technique.name: Brute Force
matched_technique.justification: Alert indicators (RDP Failed Logins Threshold Exceeded; rules: Auth: Multiple Login Failures From Single Source) overlap most strongly with T1110 Brute Force among the ATT&CK shortlist.

priority.reasoning: Scored High based on: QRadar magnitude 7/10; source is external to the network; credibility 7/10.

next_steps: Identify the targeted account(s) and whether any authentication succeeded.
If a login succeeded, treat as a confirmed compromise and reset credentials immediately.
Enforce/verify account lockout and MFA on the targeted service.
Block the source IP and hunt for the same source against other assets.
triage.suggested_query:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where IpAddress == "98.79.243.215" and EventID in (4625, 4624)
| summarize Failures=countif(EventID==4625), Successes=countif(EventID==4624) by Account, Computer

raw_details.description: Directory Traversal Against Web Server :: Web Attack targeting vpn-gw-01

triage.summary: Likely web attack: Directory Traversal Against Web Server from external source 156.240.2.115 against vpn-gw-01 (10.20.1.2) over HTTP, 214 events observed.

matched_technique.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
matched_technique.justification: Alert indicators (Directory Traversal Against Web Server; rules: Web: SQL Injection Signature Match) overlap most strongly with T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application among the ATT&CK shortlist.

priority.reasoning: Scored High based on: QRadar magnitude 7/10; source is external to the network.

next_steps: Review the raw request payload and confirm whether the application was actually vulnerable.
Check WAF/application logs for a successful response (200 with data) vs. blocked.
Validate input handling on the targeted endpoint; block the source IP.
Scan the web asset for web shells or unexpected file changes.
triage.suggested_query:
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where CIp == "156.240.2.115"
| project TimeGenerated, CsMethod, CsUriStem, ScStatus, CsUriQuery
